canonical_definition: Based on the provided sources, GSM-symbolic is a benchmark dataset used to evaluate reasoning in large language models. It is defined as a collection of math word problems where names and numerical values are replaced with symbolic variables. The task for a model is to generate the correct symbolic expression as a solution. The available literature consistently presents this instrument as a tool designed to assess LLMs’ “mathematical reasoning skills.” One source also characterizes it as a “challenging symbolic reasoning benchmark.” Therefore, within this set of papers, the operationalization of mathematical reasoning is the successful generation of symbolic solutions to these modified word problems.
papers: AdaptiveStep: Automatically Dividing Reasoning Step through Model Confidence
CRANE: Reasoning with constrained LLM generation
Position: Formal Mathematical Reasoning—A New Frontier in AI

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset of math word problems where names and numerical values are replaced with symbolic variables, used to assess mathematical reasoning by requiring the generation of correct symbolic expressions.
definitions.paper: CRANE: Reasoning with constrained LLM generation

relationships.paper: GSM-Symbolic: Understanding the Limitations of Mathematical Reasoning in Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: "GSM-Symbolic (Mirzadeh et al., 2024), a dataset consisting of math word problems designed to assess LLMs’ mathematical reasoning skills"

icml25: CRANE: Reasoning with constrained LLM generation
